name: SDOT Street Use Sign-In Data
provider: City of Seattle Department of Transportation Street Use Division

columns.name: ServiceTime
columns.description: Calculated field: TimeCompletedTime minus TimeReachedTime in minutes.

columns.name: ExcludeWait
columns.description: Calculated field: Exclude from wait time calculations. Calculates TimeReachedTime minus TimeInTime and assumes that if this is greater than 9 hours (the number of hours the counter is open), someone did not click a button and the data is in error. Also returns "Exclude" if the TimeReachedTime is blank.

columns.name: Meeting
columns.description: Calculated field: Time waiting for meetings does not reflect applicant services team performance. Exclude meetings from calculations.

columns.name: ExcludeService
columns.description: Calculated field: Exclude from service time calculations. Calculates TimeCompletedTime minus TimeReachedTime and assumes that if this is greater than 9 hours (the number of hours the counter is open), someone did not click a button and the data is in error. Also returns "Exclude" if the TimeCompletedTime is blank.

columns.name: Week
columns.description: Calculated field: Week number where Monday is day 1 of the week. Some years end up with 53 weeks and some have no year 1.

columns.name: TimeCompletedDate
columns.description: Raw data: Date customer finished working with permit reviewer.

columns.name: MeetWith
columns.description: Raw data: If the service category is "Meeting," this is the Street Use or Urban Forestry employee that the visitor is meeting with.

columns.name: ServiceHours
columns.description: Calculated field: Calculates TimeCompletedTime minus TimeReachedTime in hours. Used for calculating service times longer than 2 hours.

columns.name: WaitHours
columns.description: Calculated field: Calculates TimeReachedTime minus TimeInTime. Used to calculate wait times greater than two hours.

columns.name: >2Hours
columns.description: Calculated field: Was the customer's wait or service time greater than 2 hours?

columns.name: WaitTime
columns.description: Calculated field: TimeReachedTime minus TimeInTime in minutes.

columns.name: VISIT_ID
columns.description: Raw data: Unique ID for visit. Primary Key.

columns.name: VRL_VISIT_REASON
columns.description: Raw data: Visit reasons updated in July 2013 and February 2014. Prior to February 2014, visitors self-selected. Post February 2014, qualified screeners made the selections. Data unreliable prior to February 2014. Exclude Staff Use Only -- this checks visitors in for screening. After screening, customers are signed back in for the a specific visit reason. Counting both would inflate visit counts. Visitors may check in more than once for other categories since they may need to see multiple reviewer types depending on the complexity of their project(s).

columns.name: TimeInDate
columns.description: Raw data: Date customer signed into system.

columns.name: TimeInTime
columns.description: Raw data: Time customer signed into system. Start of Wait Time. (Socrata does not recognize this as a time field. Format: h:mm:ss AM/PM)

columns.name: TimeReachedDate
columns.description: Raw data: Date customer reached by permit reviewer.

columns.name: TimeReachedTime
columns.description: Raw data: Time customer reached by permit reviewer. End of Wait time. Start of Service time. (Socrata does not recognize this as a time field. Format: h:mm:ss AM/PM)

columns.name: TimeCompletedTime
columns.description: Raw data: Time customer finished with permit reviewer. End of Service Time. (Socrata does not recognize this as a time field. Format: h:mm:ss AM/PM)

columns.name: VRL_VISIT_REASON_ID
columns.description: Raw data: Unique ID for Visit reason (category). Foreign key.

columns.name: VSL_STATUS
columns.description: Raw data: Status of the customer at the time the data was last uploaded. If the date is more than a day older than the data and the status is not Completed, a button was not clicked. All visitors leave Street Use by 5:00 PM each day.

description: Data from Seattle Department of Transportation Street Use Division Applicant Services Sign In system. Data collection started in May 2009. Data is used to run the SUSI application, which alerts permit reviewers by type of review that an applicant is at the counter. Applicants come to the counter to pay fees, get coaching, apply for permits, and extend permits for a wide variety of private uses of the right of way. The data can be used to calculate changes over time for visit volumes, volumes by

columns.name: Share of hours completed by workers from economically distressed ZIP codes

description: In 2015, the City began implementing a master Community Workforce Agreement (CWA). The agreement prioritizes the hiring of residents from economically distressed neighborhoods of Seattle and King County, women, and people of color on City construction projects over $5 million. This data set reflects the percentage of annual priority hire hours worked by residents living in economically distressed ZIP codes.
